experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active T near the top in its spawn orientation. With no stack or immediate threats, the priority is efficient placement and preserving flexibility for upcoming I,Z,L,O pieces. Candidate moves: left/right would only shift horizontally without making progress; rotations are unnecessary on an empty board; no_op also wastes a turn. Dropping downward is best, and on an empty board a hard drop safely places the T immediately to start building a flat base and maximize tempo.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active I piece is horizontal near the top, while a T piece is resting at the bottom center. The board is otherwise empty, so the main goal is to place the I efficiently to build a flat stack and preserve flexibility for upcoming Z, L, O, S pieces. Dropping the horizontal I now can land it cleanly on top of the T area, creating a broad platform without introducing vertical holes; rotating vertical is unnecessary on an open board, and lateral moves are less urgent than securing the placement immediately. Therefore the best action is to hard drop.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top in horizontal orientation, with only a T and I stack low on the board and no immediate danger. Goal is to place the Z efficiently without creating extra holes; the best sub-goal is to move it toward the left wall so it can be dropped flat and preserve the center/right for upcoming L,O,S,J. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would leave it near the center top, less flexible; rotate_right makes it vertical without a clear need; right shifts into a less useful area; soft_drop commits downward too early. Moving left improves future placement while keeping options open.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top; stack at the bottom has a flat I row with a T shape creating a small center bump. Immediate threats are low since the board is mostly empty. Goal is to place the Z to complement the bottom structure rather than locking it high. Among actions, horizontal movement or rotation can prepare placement, but the most direct progress now is to bring the piece downward while preserving flexibility for later alignment with the center-right surface. Hard drop would commit too early near the top path is clear but may not be optimal without horizontal adjustment; soft drop advances safely one step and keeps options open.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is Z near the top with room to maneuver; stack below has an I on row 18 and a T structure on rows 19-20, leaving open space above. Goal is to place the Z to complement the uneven right-side T/I surface and avoid leaving isolated holes. Immediate options: moving left keeps it over flatter empty space with little support; rotating now makes a vertical zigzag that could later slot beside the T notch; moving right also heads toward the existing structure but without changing shape yet. Since there is ample height and the rotated orientation is more versatile for fitting around the lower T shape, best sub-goal is to orient first while safe. Action: rotate_right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the upper-middle, with a T and horizontal I stack already on the bottom creating a modest central mound. Immediate threat is low because the board is mostly empty, so the goal is better long-term placement rather than urgency. A useful sub-goal is to avoid leaving awkward overhangs and to move the Z toward a side where upcoming L,O,S,J pieces can stack cleanly. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit too early in a poor central spot; soft_drop advances but without improving alignment; rotations only switch between the two Z orientations and current orientation is already fine. Moving left shifts the Z away from the center mound and toward a cleaner placement lane, which is the best preparatory move now.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top with ample space. The stack is low and uneven mainly on the left/center bottom, with no immediate threat. Goal is to improve future placement flexibility and avoid committing the L high in its current spawn orientation. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would place it immediately without horizontal adjustment, likely wasting flexibility; no_op also delays without benefit. Moving right heads toward the flatter center/right above the I and O columns, giving better future tuck options than moving left into the taller left structure. Rotation can wait until after shifting because there is no pressure. Best immediate sub-goal is to translate the piece right while still high.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I horizontally at the top around columns 4-7 with open space below. The stack is low but uneven, with a vertical well near column 2 and some flat support around row 17. Goal is to place the I efficiently to keep the surface clean and preserve flexibility for upcoming T,Z,S,O. Rotating right would make a vertical I at column 6, but dropping there onto the existing row-17 I would create a tall spike and not clear lines. Moving left/right first could set up a better placement later, but with one action now the strongest immediate progress is to rotate so the I can potentially fit a vertical channel on subsequent moves; however current board has more value in simply advancing downward while keeping horizontal options. Soft drop safely progresses toward landing without committing sideways prematurely.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I vertically at column 4 with open space beneath until it would rest on the stack around row 10. The board surface is uneven, but there is a clear 4-wide horizontal gap on row 17 at columns 3-6 that could be filled by laying the I flat, improving the surface without blocking future placements. Goal is to place this I efficiently and set up the upcoming T/Z/S/O pieces. Candidate actions: hard/soft drop now would lock the I vertically in a mediocre central column; left/right alone only shifts; rotate_left/right changes to horizontal, which is the key first step toward that better placement. So rotate_right is best now.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I tetromino currently horizontal near the top, spanning columns 2-5 around row 9, with vertical rotation valid at about column 4. The stack below has a clean 4-wide horizontal gap on row 18 at columns 3-6 already occupied by an older I, but no immediate Tetris well; the upper field is mostly open, so the main opportunity is to place this I efficiently without creating awkward overhangs. Rotating vertical now would set up a potential drop into a single column, but there is no obvious deep 4-cell vertical well beneath the current x-position. Keeping it horizontal and dropping can likely fill across the open upper surface around the left-middle without worsening the stack. Among candidate actions, left/right only reposition without commitment, rotate_right changes to a less immediately useful vertical orientation, soft_drop is slower, and hard_drop efficiently places the flat I onto the current surface in a safe, low-risk way.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top center in spawn orientation over a fairly uneven stack with the highest structures on the left and center-left, while the right side is relatively open. Immediate threat is low because the piece is at the top with plenty of vertical space, so the goal is to position it toward a cleaner landing zone rather than drop prematurely onto the central bumps. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would commit too early onto a messy center; rotations are not yet necessary because T in flat orientation can still be moved later and there is open space; left moves toward taller clutter; right moves toward the more open right side and preserves future options for placing the T efficiently. Therefore move right.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near the center-right with plenty of empty space below. Current stack is low and uneven, with no immediate threat, so the priority is efficient placement rather than urgency. The best sub-goal is to move the T toward useful future fitting over the central cavities; since it is still very high, horizontal repositioning now is cheap and preserves options better than dropping immediately. Rotating doesn’t create a clear advantage yet, and hard/soft drop would commit too early. Shifting left starts bringing the piece toward the board’s more relevant central-left structure.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high and centered above a relatively uneven stack. The immediate opportunity is to place it to improve surface shape without creating new overhangs; there is no urgent top-out threat yet. Below, around rows 8-10, there is support under columns 3-6 from the I piece, and dropping the current flat T orientation where it is would settle neatly with the stem filling center space, giving a fairly even top and preserving flexibility for upcoming Z,S,O,J. Candidate moves: left/right would shift before assessing exact fit, but current x-position already aligns with the broad central support; rotations create taller protrusions and less evenness. soft_drop only delays. hard_drop efficiently locks in the favorable placement now.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is Z near the top, currently horizontal with columns around 4-6. The stack is low with a notable left-center notch around rows 14-16, and no immediate danger from height. Goal now is efficient placement to maintain a flat surface and potentially fit upcoming S/O/J pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop would place the horizontal Z on the upper surface immediately but likely less precisely than setting up the vertical orientation; soft_drop waits but misses a chance to align better; left/right could reposition, but rotating first opens more placement options into the central notch. Rotating right turns the Z vertical, which is the most promising setup for dropping into uneven terrain next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top in vertical orientation at columns 5-6 with no immediate collision beneath for many rows. The stack is uneven but not threatening at the top, and a flat placement is usually better for Z than leaving it vertical. Candidate actions: soft_drop/hard_drop would commit a poor vertical placement high over the center-right; left/right just shift without improving shape yet. Rotating right would change the Z to horizontal orientation, creating better future placement options before dropping. Goal now is to orient the piece efficiently while there is ample space.

Directly hard-dropping from the current horizontal orientation likely lands awkwardly on the left-middle surface and may create more unevenness, while rotating to vertical gives more flexibility to fit into notches. A rotate_right now changes the Z into vertical orientation safely in open space, improving future placement options before committing to lateral movement or drop.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top in vertical orientation, with open space below and a somewhat uneven stack. Immediate threats are low because the piece is high and there is no time pressure from near-top out. Goal is to place the S efficiently to reduce surface roughness and avoid creating isolated holes. Comparing actions: rotating left/right would switch to horizontal, but there is no nearby support yet and moving first is more flexible. Soft/hard drop now would commit high above the stack, likely wasting placement options. The right side under the piece has a Z/S bump around columns 5-7 that could accept an S after shifting; moving right once improves alignment with that contour while preserving future rotation/drop choices. So the best current sub-goal is lateral positioning to the right.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top-right, currently horizontal at rows 3-4 around columns 5-7. Surface below has a notch around columns 5-7 on the upper stack; immediate danger is low since the board top is mostly empty. Goal is to place this S efficiently to reduce gaps and keep the stack flat for upcoming O/J/O/S pieces. Candidate moves: hard_drop would commit the current horizontal S, likely landing across the uneven area but may not fit the notch optimally; left/right could reposition before dropping; rotate_right makes the S vertical, which better matches the visible cavity shape around the upper-right/center and preserves flatter stacking. Soft_drop is premature before deciding orientation. Best sub-goal now is to orient the piece to fit future placement options, so rotate_right is strongest.

experiences.intentions: Board is quite stacked in the lower half with a flat-ish center around rows 17-19 and no immediate top-out danger. The active piece appears to be O already at the top, and the rotation previews are unchanged, confirming rotation is irrelevant. Goal is to place the O cleanly without creating holes, ideally fitting the existing 2-wide gap near the upper center by moving it horizontally into a safe column before dropping. Candidate actions: rotate_left/right do nothing useful; soft/hard drop now would lock the O in its current upper-center columns, which is acceptable but gives up possible positioning; no_op also wastes a move. Moving left shifts the O toward a cleaner central placement and keeps options open for the upcoming J,O,S,I sequence.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is O at the very top, already in its only orientation, sitting around columns 3-4. The stack below is uneven but there is open space directly beneath the O piece in those columns; no immediate line clear is available from a lateral move this turn, and rotating has no effect for O. Goal is to place the O efficiently without wasting time, preserving flexibility for upcoming J,O,S,I. Candidate actions: left/right would only shift the O and may misalign it with existing gaps; rotate actions are irrelevant; soft_drop advances but not maximally. Hard dropping immediately settles the O into the open slot below, making progress safely.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the top with plenty of horizontal freedom. The stack is uneven but not immediately threatening; there is a notable left-side well/opening above the lower L/J structure, while the center-right is already cluttered. Goal now is to improve surface and keep options open for upcoming O,S,I,Z pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit too early from spawn and likely create awkward height; soft_drop is premature before horizontal alignment; rotations mostly make the J vertical without clear immediate benefit. Moving left begins steering the J toward the left-side cavity/ledge, which is the most promising placement and preserves future choices.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is near the top with plenty of space. The stack is uneven but not in immediate danger; the most notable open structure is around columns 3-5 in upper-middle, and preserving flexibility matters because next pieces are O,S,I,Z. Current spawn orientation is flat J with the foot on the left; rotating right to vertical creates a cleaner narrow profile that can be moved/dropped into gaps more easily on the next turn. Hard/soft drop now would commit too early from spawn height without lateral adjustment, and left/right first is less generally useful than getting the piece into its more maneuverable orientation. Goal is to place J efficiently while avoiding creating overhangs; sub-goal now is improve mobility and future fit. Therefore rotate_right is best.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is O at the very top centered columns 4-5, with empty space directly below for several rows. An O piece has no meaningful rotation, so rotate actions do nothing useful. Immediate goal is to place it to improve the surface and possibly support future S/I/Z/T pieces. The board has a left-side notch around row 11-13 and a relatively busy center-right; moving the O left starts aligning it toward lower open columns and avoids committing to the crowded center. hard/soft drop now would just place it high in the middle, which is weak. A single left is the best setup action this turn.
